evidence_excerpt: Despite symptomatic improvement on L-threo-DOPS, he "remained susceptible to occasional collapse" and ultimately "was found halfway dressed and could not be resuscitated."

quality_of_life.functional_status_evidence_excerpt: Orthostatic hypotension improved considerably on L-threo-DOPS, yet he remained prone to collapse, indicating persistent limitations affecting normal activities.
quality_of_life.symptom_burden_evidence_excerpt: Even with treatment, he "remained susceptible to occasional collapse."

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: He was resistant to fludrocortisone; orthostatic hypotension improved considerably on L-threo-3,4-dihydroxyphenylserine (DOPS).
Orthostatic hypotension improved considerably on L-threo-DOPS.
One morning he was found halfway dressed and could not be resuscitated.

notes_on_uncertainty: The exact date of definitive diagnosis and initiation of therapy is not provided; the proxy reference is the start of the 4-year specialist follow-up. Specific timing of treatment changes and symptom improvement is not documented, so those events are assigned an unknown time period. Long-term outcome is clear due to documented death.
